summary: S9 now includes response-head pre-read diagnostics, concurrent phase diagnostics, rejected OpenSSL release-buffers, explicit HTTP/1 flush, ylong runtime-worker=8 and runtime-worker=1 candidates, a rejected single-driver parent-future polling candidate, a superseded concurrency=8 bench_tls_io diagnostic, retained dynamic request-budget fairness correction, server-side send/relay timing, a tokio_base dynamic-budget candidate, refreshed real-client-path evidence, and an strace syscall-shape diagnostic. Strace/source review refutes a simple network syscall-count explanation and points to ylong_runtime scheduling cost, but worker-count tuning and parent-future single-driver polling both fail SOTA gates and are not retained. S9 remains active.

source_changes.summary: Adds RequestRefEncoder, a borrowed HTTP/1 request encoder that preserves RequestEncoder wire bytes while avoiding RequestPart/header-map cloning.
Re-exports RequestRefEncoder from the HTTP/1 request module.
Re-exports RequestRefEncoder from ylong_http::h1.
Async HTTP/1 request sending uses RequestRefEncoder, skips empty request-body encoding, and uses a fixed 16 KiB buffer instead of per-request Vec allocation.
Body::is_empty is available outside http2 so HTTP/1 can fast-path empty request bodies.
Replaces Cursor<Vec<u8>> response pre-read storage with a small PreRead helper for Text, Chunk, and UntilClose bodies, with a regression test for drain behavior.
Adds bench_tls_io-only TLS I/O counter snapshots used to measure SSL read/write calls and underlying stream pending counts.
Instruments AsyncSslStream poll_read and poll_write with benchmark-only SSL call and WouldBlock counters.
Instruments the OpenSSL wrapper BIO with benchmark-only underlying read/write call and Pending counters.
Adds opt-in benchmark-only phase counters for request_format, pool_checkout, send_on_conn, HTTP/1 write split, and response-head split.

HTTP/1 pool checkout now scans the dispatcher list in place and removes shutdown entries without replacing the Vec, preserving retained capacity across hot-path reuse; a regression test proves the old capacity-loss behavior.
Adds libcurl_bench-only response-head poll and pending counters used to test whether response waits are caused by repeated polling.

Benchmark phase timing is opt-in through YLONG_BENCH_PHASES, prints HTTP/1 encode/write-I/O plus response read poll/pending/intercept/decode counters, reuses the ylong body-drain buffer across requests, and can run under ylong_base or tokio_base.

Relaxed the https_proxy_bench required features so the same benchmark binary can be built with either ylong_base or tokio_base, and adds bench_tls_io so TLS diagnostics do not contaminate canonical libcurl_bench builds.
Re-exports TLS diagnostic snapshot helpers only when bench_tls_io is enabled and provides no-op fallbacks otherwise.

Harness parses finer phase timing columns, records proxy/origin request-shape byte counters, records request_body_bytes metadata, and supports result/figure directories so diagnostics do not overwrite canonical outputs.
Adds tests for finer phase parsing, trace request-shape parsing, opt-in phase env, result-dir output, figure-dir output, and benchmark fixture behavior.
Updates the checked-in fair matrix and explicitly keeps it as non-SOTA counterexample evidence.
Sync connector now sets TCP_NODELAY through a shared connect_tcp helper, with a regression test.
Sync HTTP/1 pool checkout now preserves dispatcher-list Vec capacity, matching the async capacity fix.
Benchmark binary can explicitly select ylong_http_client_sync as a separate public-client candidate while keeping all/default canonical async selection unchanged.
Adds YLONG_BENCH_YLONG_CONCURRENCY_MODEL=threaded|single-client. Default threaded preserves existing behavior; single-client explicitly runs concurrency > 1 through one shared async public Client.
Adds --ylong-concurrency-model and groups results/comparisons by ylong_concurrency_model to prevent threaded and single-client diagnostics from being merged.
Adds regression coverage for single-client concurrency-model environment wiring and comparison grouping.
Adds YlongBenchConfig so async ylong benchmark clients match max_h1_conn_number to benchmark concurrency for fair single-client/multi comparisons while preserving default behavior at concurrency=1.

Adds SharedRequestBudget so single-client async benchmark workers dynamically claim one request at a time, matching libcurl multi's complete-and-refill model more closely than fixed split_work partitions.
For single-client async concurrency, samples global bench_tls_io counters once in the outer coordinator instead of collecting and summing one global delta per worker.
Adds benchmark-only body chunk/byte counters to BenchRun, async/sync body drain paths, concurrent aggregation, and same-process libcurl write callback state.
Parses *_body_stats lines and records body_chunks/body_bytes in raw and summary benchmark CSVs.
Adds regression coverage for body stats parsing and summary aggregation.
Records server-observed proxy/origin TLS fingerprints after handshake and propagates them through trace output, result CSVs, and summary CSVs.
Adds regression coverage for TLS fingerprint parsing, summary aggregation, and repeated same-fingerprint delta counting.
Adds libcurl_bench-only response_pre_read_bytes and response_pre_read_events counters with accumulation and getters.
Records rem.len() when HTTP/1 response-head decode succeeds while phase timing is enabled.
Aggregates and prints response pre-read byte/event counters in _phase_us output.
Parses, writes, and summarizes response_pre_read_bytes/events phase fields.
Adds regression coverage for response pre-read phase parsing and summary columns.
Records proxy direct-response send time, proxy tunnel send-to-client/send-to-origin relay time, and origin response send time, then propagates them through raw, summary, and comparison CSVs.
Adds regression coverage for parsing and summarizing proxy/origin server-side timing fields.

available_causal_support: Canonical threshold run uses public ylong async ClientBuilder, Proxy builder, request builder, client.request, and response body drain path.
Same-process libcurl baseline reuses one Easy handle across warmup and measured requests.
Canonical threshold CSVs have phase_timing=false and zero phase columns; diagnostic phase CSVs are isolated under docs/benchmarks/results/phase-diagnostic/.
Server-side trace parity holds: one proxy connection per repeat; HTTPS-origin scenarios use one CONNECT tunnel, one origin connection, and one origin TLS connection per repeat.
The benchmark records request_body_bytes=0 and response_body_bytes=4096, so empty-body and request-encoding optimizations are measured-path optimizations but do not prove upload-heavy performance.
RequestRefEncoder is covered by a ylong_http unit test that compares borrowed and owned encoder bytes for origin-form, absolute-form, and combined header values.
Opt-in phase timing now separates HTTP/1 encode from socket write I/O and response read from interceptor/decode work.
Latest phase diagnostic shows parser/encoder costs are small relative to socket write and response read/wait costs, creating a counterexample to encoder-only SOTA explanations.
Proxy/origin trace counters now record request header bytes, request body bytes, and response body bytes, showing ylong and libcurl exercise the same response payload size and zero request bodies.
The ylong benchmark now reuses its response drain buffer across requests so the canonical matrix does not include a per-request 16 KiB benchmark-side buffer initialization artifact.
The same public benchmark path now builds under tokio_base, which improves the best observed full-matrix point estimate but still refutes SOTA completion.
The HttpBody PreRead helper removes Cursor<Vec<u8>> from response pre-read body handling, but fresh ylong_base and tokio_base matrices still refute SOTA and show this is not a SOTA-scale lever.
TLS I/O diagnostics are isolated behind bench_tls_io so canonical libcurl_bench fairness builds do not compile diagnostic counter code.

TLS I/O diagnostics show no wrapper poll explosion: HTTP over HTTPS proxy averages 2 SSL reads and 1 SSL-read pending per measured request; HTTPS-origin proxy scenarios average 5 SSL reads and 2 SSL-read pendings per request.
The HTTP/1 pool capacity test is a true red/green regression: before the fix, exist_h1_conn dropped list capacity from 65 to 4; after the fix, repeated reuse preserves the reserved capacity.
The response-poll diagnostic shows each measured response head averages exactly 2 polls and 1 pending, so the response wait is not a repeated-poll/wakeup storm.
The public sync ClientBuilder + Client::request path can now be benchmarked explicitly as ylong_http_client_sync without redefining the canonical async ylong_http_client matrix.

The sync candidate improves CPU/request geomean to 0.738x of libcurl, but fails fixed SOTA gates with 1.024x throughput geomean, 0.718x worst throughput, p50/p95 regressions, a CPU failing cell, and RSS up to 1.066x.
The benchmark can now explicitly distinguish threaded ylong concurrency from a single-runtime, single-public-Client ylong async concurrency model without changing defaults.

The single-client concurrency candidate refutes a benchmark-model-only explanation for the prior concurrent failure: even with one public ylong client, throughput geomean is 0.895x of libcurl, worst throughput is 0.676x, CPU/request geomean is 1.588x, and RSS reaches 1.176x.

The worst single-client concurrent cell is proxy-mtls-https-origin at 1000 requests, reinforcing that the remaining SOTA-scale problem is concentrated in HTTPS origin over HTTPS proxy and proxy mTLS nested TLS paths rather than client construction alone.

The fairness-corrected single-client max-H1 candidate confirms same-concurrency trace parity: both ylong and libcurl use 8 proxy connections; HTTPS-origin scenarios use 8 CONNECT tunnels, 8 origin connections, and 8 origin TLS connections.

The max-H1 candidate improves single-client throughput geomean from 0.895x to 1.022x, but still fails fixed SOTA gates with 0.676x worst throughput, p50 max 1.488x, p95 max 1.378x, CPU/request geomean 1.305x, and RSS max 1.190x.

The worst max-H1 concurrent cell is https-over-https-proxy at 1000 requests, and proxy-mTLS HTTPS-origin at 1000 requests is also 0.699x, reinforcing that the remaining SOTA-scale problem is nested TLS over CONNECT rather than HTTP-over-proxy forwarding.

Body-drain chunk/byte diagnostics show ylong_http_client and libcurl both produce exactly one measured body chunk per request and exactly 4096 body bytes per request in all fair single-client max-H1 scenarios.

The body-stats diagnostic refutes body chunk fragmentation as the remaining SOTA-scale cause; HTTPS-origin over HTTPS proxy still fails throughput, p50/p95, CPU, and RSS gates despite identical body callback shape.

Server-observed TLS fingerprint diagnostics show ylong_http_client and libcurl both negotiate TLSv1.3:TLS_AES_256_GCM_SHA384:256 on every proxy TLS connection and every origin TLS connection in the fair single-client max-H1 matrix.

The TLS fingerprint diagnostic refutes TLS version/cipher mismatch as the remaining nested TLS performance cause; the remaining SOTA-scale work is in ylong's nested TLS implementation path rather than benchmark TLS parameter fairness.

A dedicated AsyncSslStream<AsyncSslStream<TcpStream>> HTTPS-over-secure-proxy candidate was tested and rejected; it worsened throughput geomean to 0.913x and worst throughput to 0.650x while increasing CPU/RSS costs.
The rejected nested TLS variant refutes ProxyStream enum dispatch as a sufficient SOTA-scale root cause and was not retained in the production code path.
tokio_base single-client max-H1 reduces CPU/request geomean to 0.841x of libcurl but still fails throughput, p50/p95, and RSS SOTA gates.

The worst tokio_base concurrent cell is proxy-mTLS HTTPS-origin at 1000 requests with 0.790x throughput, reinforcing that proxy mTLS nested TLS remains the hardest path even when runtime substrate improves.
Response pre-read diagnostics show ylong_http_client pre-reads exactly 4096 body bytes and one pre-read event per measured request in every single-concurrency phase scenario.
The same diagnostic shows body_drain is already about 0.9-1.0us/request, so PreRead storage/copy alone is not a SOTA-scale explanation for the remaining gap.
Because phase timing is intentionally restricted to concurrency=1, response-preread-diagnostic is causal narrowing evidence and cannot replace the fixed concurrency=8 SOTA threshold matrices.

Concurrent phase diagnostics at concurrency=8 show response_read accounts for about 95% of request_execute time in the failing HTTPS-origin proxy cells, while pool_checkout is about 3us/request and body_drain is about 1.5us/request.

The concurrent phase diagnostic narrows the next implementation target to nested TLS response-read or runtime scheduling around it; CONNECT parsing, request formatting, connection-pool checkout, and body-drain storage are not sufficient SOTA levers.
Corrected concurrent bench_tls_io diagnostics keep the top-level HTTP response read shape stable at exactly 2 polls and 1 pending per measured ylong request.

The corrected concurrent bench_tls_io diagnostic refutes the earlier inflated nested TLS call-count explanation: HTTPS-over-HTTPS-proxy 1000 and proxy-mTLS HTTPS-origin 1000 both average 5 SSL_read calls and 2 SSL-read pendings per measured request.
HTTP-over-HTTPS-proxy 1000 averages 2 SSL_read calls and 1 SSL-read pending per request, so nested HTTPS-origin proxy still has more TLS work than HTTP-over-proxy but not a call-count explosion.

Server-side timing diagnostics show ylong nested HTTPS-origin cells have slower proxy/origin send/relay timing than libcurl, but the delta is too small to explain the full 1.26-1.31 ms/request response_read cost.

tokio_base dynamic-budget results improve throughput geomean to 1.133x and CPU/request geomean to 0.779x, but still fail SOTA with 0.809x worst throughput and nested p50/p95 regressions; 1.073x max RSS is above libcurl but within the <=1.10x hard threshold.
An explicit async HTTP/1 flush before response read can be tested and made observable in a fake stream, but the full fair matrix shows it is not a SOTA-scale performance lever.

An explicit ylong_base 8-worker runtime benchmark configuration improves HTTP-over-HTTPS-proxy throughput, but the full matrix still fails SOTA with 1.006x throughput geomean, 0.686x worst throughput, nested HTTPS-origin p50/p95 regressions, 1.269x CPU/request geomean, and 1.165x max RSS.

A ylong_base 1-worker runtime benchmark configuration improves the HTTPS-over-HTTPS-proxy 200-request cell, but the focused 1000-request cell still fails with 0.863x throughput, 1.172x p50, and 1.111x p95 ratios, so worker-count tuning is not a SOTA-scale solution.

A benchmark-only single-driver parent future that directly polls concurrent public Client::request futures was tested and removed. It improves CPU/request geomean to 0.709x of libcurl on the focused HTTPS-over-HTTPS-proxy matrix, but fails throughput and tail gates with 0.951x throughput geomean, 0.823x worst throughput, 1.212x p50 max, and 1.098x p95 max.

A strace -f syscall-shape diagnostic on HTTPS-over-HTTPS-proxy refutes a simple network syscall-count explanation: ylong has comparable recvfrom/sendto counts to libcurl, while futex, clock_nanosleep, epoll_wait, and sched_yield activity is much higher under tracing. Because strace distorts timings, this is causal narrowing evidence rather than threshold evidence.

phase_diagnostic_dataset.command: PATH="$HOME/.cargo/bin:$PATH" python3 docs/benchmarks/run_https_proxy_bench.py --baseline libcurl --scenario all --requests 200 --repeats 3 --warmup 20 --bench-bin /tmp/ylong_http_target_phase_release/release/https_proxy_bench --phase-timing --result-dir docs/benchmarks/results/phase-diagnostic --figure-dir docs/benchmarks/results/phase-diagnostic
diagnosis: HTTP/1 request encoding is about 1.0-1.1 us/request in the measured ylong path, only a small fraction of the HTTP/1 write phase.
HTTP/1 socket write I/O is about 19-22 us/request and accounts for roughly 91-93% of the HTTP/1 write phase.
response_read accounts for roughly 95-98% of response_head; response decode is about 3 us/request and interceptor time is about 0.1 us/request.
The benchmark drain buffer is now reused across requests; body_drain remains about 1 us/request and is not a SOTA-scale lever.

Trace-shape counters show ylong sends no more request body bytes and no larger response body than libcurl; request header bytes are smaller for ylong in the local fixture, so request size does not explain the remaining SOTA gap.
The full non-phase matrix remains the authoritative SOTA threshold dataset; phase timing is diagnostic only.

canonical_threshold_dataset.baseline: same-process libcurl with one reused Easy handle across warmup and measured requests
canonical_threshold_dataset.origin_tls: local CA verified HTTPS origin for HTTPS-origin scenarios
canonical_threshold_dataset.mTLS: proxy client certificate/key enabled for proxy-mtls-https-origin

runtime_candidate_dataset.command: PATH="$HOME/.cargo/bin:$PATH" python3 docs/benchmarks/run_https_proxy_bench.py --baseline libcurl --scenario all --requests 200,1000,3000 --repeats 5 --warmup 50 --bench-bin /tmp/ylong_http_target_tokio_release/release/https_proxy_bench --result-dir docs/benchmarks/results/tokio-full --figure-dir docs/benchmarks/results/tokio-full

tls_io_diagnostic_dataset.command: PATH="$HOME/.cargo/bin:$PATH" python3 docs/benchmarks/run_https_proxy_bench.py --baseline libcurl --scenario all --requests 200 --repeats 3 --warmup 20 --phase-timing --bench-bin /tmp/ylong_http_target_tlsdiag_ylong_release/release/https_proxy_bench --result-dir docs/benchmarks/results/tls-io-diagnostic --figure-dir docs/benchmarks/results/tls-io-diagnostic
tls_io_diagnostic_dataset.canonical_fairness_note: Canonical libcurl_bench builds omit bench_tls_io; TLS counters are opt-in diagnostics only.
tls_io_diagnostic_dataset.diagnostic_conclusion: TLS wrapper poll explosion is refuted as a SOTA-scale bottleneck. The dominant measured ylong phase remains response_read within send_on_conn, with only one SSL-read pending per HTTP-over-proxy request and two SSL-read pendings per HTTPS-origin request.

same_concurrency_diagnostic.baseline: same-process libcurl multi API with maxconnects/max-host/max-total connection limits set to concurrency

causal_conclusion: The borrowed encoder and empty-body/fixed-buffer optimizations are real-client-path changes; response drain-buffer reuse removes a benchmark-side allocation artifact without bypassing body reads.
They improve the canonical matrix but do not reach SOTA: throughput geomean is 0.991x and the worst cell is 0.769x.
A tokio_base candidate improves the best observed full matrix to 1.065x throughput geomean and 0.862x worst cell, but still fails the fixed SOTA thresholds.

The PreRead response pre-buffer helper is a real-path micro-optimization, but its full matrices still fail SOTA: ylong_base reaches 1.013x geomean and tokio_base reaches 1.068x geomean with a 0.691x worst cell.
TLS I/O diagnostics are now isolated behind bench_tls_io; canonical libcurl_bench builds omit the counters, and the opt-in diagnostic refutes wrapper poll explosion as the next SOTA-scale lever.

The same-concurrency diagnostic uses libcurl multi rather than multi-threaded Easy handles, records concurrency as a comparison dimension, and is kept opt-in so it cannot redefine the canonical matrix.
The same-concurrency diagnostic is also a counterexample: ylong reaches only 0.842x throughput geomean at concurrency=8 while using 2.065x CPU/request and up to 1.842x RSS.

The H1 pool capacity candidate fixes a real allocation-retention bug, but the 200/1000 x 3 matrix is also a counterexample: 0.921x throughput geomean, 0.632x worst cell, p50/p95 gates fail, and RSS remains higher than libcurl.
The response-poll diagnostic refutes repeated wakeup churn: every scenario averages exactly two response-head polls and one pending per request.
The TLS fingerprint diagnostic refutes TLS version/cipher mismatch: ylong_http_client and libcurl both negotiate TLSv1.3:TLS_AES_256_GCM_SHA384:256 on proxy and origin TLS legs.
The dedicated nested TLS stream variant was tested and rejected because it worsened the matrix; it should not be used as a SOTA path.
Current causal evidence therefore refutes SOTA completion rather than supporting it.
S10 final counterexample review must not be run as a completion gate until a SOTA-passing matrix exists.

verification_commands.command: PATH="$HOME/.cargo/bin:$PATH" cargo check -p ylong_http_client --bin https_proxy_bench --no-default-features --features async,http1_1,ylong_base,c_openssl_3_0
phase diagnostic command listed in phase_diagnostic_dataset.command

verification_commands.observed: all scenario/client/repeat runs ok; zero errors in summary

verification_commands.command: PATH="$HOME/.cargo/bin:$PATH" CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/ylong_http_target_preread_red cargo test -p ylong_http_client --no-default-features --features async,http1_1,ylong_base,c_openssl_3_0 ut_pre_read_drains_without_overread
verification_commands.observed: failed before implementation with E0433 because PreRead was not defined

verification_commands.command: PATH="$HOME/.cargo/bin:$PATH" CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/ylong_http_target_preread_green cargo test -p ylong_http_client --no-default-features --features async,http1_1,ylong_base,c_openssl_3_0 ut_pre_read_drains_without_overread

verification_commands.observed: all scenario/client/repeat runs ok; zero errors; 1.013x throughput geomean and 0.819x worst cell
all scenario/client/repeat runs ok; zero errors; 1.068x throughput geomean and 0.691x worst cell
passed with existing warnings; canonical benchmark feature set omits bench_tls_io

verification_commands.command: PATH="$HOME/.cargo/bin:$PATH" CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/ylong_http_target_tlsdiag_no_libcurl cargo check -p ylong_http_client --bin https_proxy_bench --no-default-features --features async,http1_1,ylong_base,c_openssl_3_0
TLS I/O diagnostic command listed in tls_io_diagnostic_dataset.command
verification_commands.observed: all scenario/client/repeat runs ok; TLS counts parsed into summary CSV

verification_commands.command: PATH="$HOME/.cargo/bin:$PATH" CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/ylong_http_target_pool_capacity_red cargo test -p ylong_http_client --lib --no-default-features --features async,http1_1,ylong_base,c_openssl_3_0 ut_exist_h1_conn_preserves_list_capacity -- --nocapture
verification_commands.observed: failed before implementation because exist_h1_conn replaced the Vec and lost reserved capacity

verification_commands.command: PATH="$HOME/.cargo/bin:$PATH" CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/ylong_http_target_pool_capacity_green cargo test -p ylong_http_client --lib --no-default-features --features async,http1_1,ylong_base,c_openssl_3_0 ut_exist_h1_conn_preserves_list_capacity -- --nocapture
verification_commands.observed: passed after in-place list scan implementation

verification_commands.command: Pool capacity candidate command listed in pool_capacity_candidate_dataset.command
verification_commands.observed: all scenario/client/repeat runs ok; zero errors; 0.921x throughput geomean and 0.632x worst cell
passed after TimeGroup response-read poll counters were implemented

verification_commands.command: Response-poll diagnostic command listed in response_poll_diagnostic_dataset.command
verification_commands.observed: all scenario/client/repeat runs ok; every scenario averaged 2 response-head polls and 1 pending per request

completion_effect: S9 is not complete because causal evidence currently disproves, rather than proves, SOTA. S10 final counterexample review must not be run as a completion gate until a SOTA-passing matrix exists.

async_single_client_maxh1_candidate.command: PATH="$HOME/.cargo/bin:$PATH" python3 docs/benchmarks/run_https_proxy_bench.py --baseline libcurl --ylong-client async --ylong-concurrency-model single-client --scenario all --requests 200,1000 --repeats 3 --warmup 50 --concurrency 8 --bench-bin /tmp/ylong_http_target_maxh1_release/release/https_proxy_bench --result-dir docs/benchmarks/results/async-single-client-maxh1-candidate --figure-dir docs/benchmarks/results/async-single-client-maxh1-candidate

failed_gates: throughput geomean is 1.022x, below fixed SOTA lower-bound threshold 2.00x
worst throughput cell is 0.676x, below fixed per-scenario threshold 1.50x
p50 max ratio is 1.488x, above no-regression threshold
p95 max ratio is 1.378x, above no-regression threshold
CPU/request geomean is 1.305x and max is 1.613x, so resource cost worsens
RSS max ratio is 1.190x, so memory cost worsens
sota_gate_result.fairness_result: connection trace now confirms both ylong_http_client and libcurl use 8 proxy connections, 8 CONNECT tunnels for HTTPS-origin scenarios, 8 origin connections, and 8 origin TLS connections under concurrency=8.

body_stats_diagnostic.command: PATH="$HOME/.cargo/bin:$PATH" python3 docs/benchmarks/run_https_proxy_bench.py --baseline libcurl --ylong-client async --ylong-concurrency-model single-client --scenario all --requests 200,1000 --repeats 3 --warmup 50 --concurrency 8 --bench-bin /tmp/ylong_http_target_body_stats_release/release/https_proxy_bench --result-dir docs/benchmarks/results/body-stats-diagnostic --figure-dir docs/benchmarks/results/body-stats-diagnostic
body_stats_diagnostic.finding: The body-drain chunk-shape hypothesis is refuted. Every scenario and request count shows both ylong_http_client and libcurl delivering exactly one measured body callback/data chunk per request and exactly 4096 body bytes per request. The remaining HTTPS-origin-over-HTTPS-proxy gap is not explained by benchmark-side body chunk fragmentation.

tls_fingerprint_diagnostic.command: PATH="$HOME/.cargo/bin:$PATH" python3 docs/benchmarks/run_https_proxy_bench.py --baseline libcurl --ylong-client async --ylong-concurrency-model single-client --scenario all --requests 200 --repeats 1 --warmup 50 --concurrency 8 --bench-bin /tmp/ylong_http_target_body_stats_release/release/https_proxy_bench --result-dir docs/benchmarks/results/tls-fingerprint-diagnostic --figure-dir docs/benchmarks/results/tls-fingerprint-diagnostic
tls_fingerprint_diagnostic.finding: The TLS version/cipher mismatch hypothesis is refuted. ylong_http_client and libcurl negotiate the same TLSv1.3 cipher on every proxy TLS and origin TLS connection in this fair single-client max-H1 diagnostic.

nested_tls_variant_candidate.command: PATH="$HOME/.cargo/bin:$PATH" python3 docs/benchmarks/run_https_proxy_bench.py --baseline libcurl --ylong-client async --ylong-concurrency-model single-client --scenario all --requests 200,1000 --repeats 3 --warmup 50 --concurrency 8 --bench-bin /tmp/ylong_http_target_nested_variant_release/release/https_proxy_bench --result-dir docs/benchmarks/results/nested-tls-variant-candidate --figure-dir docs/benchmarks/results/nested-tls-variant-candidate
nested_tls_variant_candidate.finding: The dedicated nested TLS stream variant is a rejected counterexample. It worsened throughput, CPU, p50/p95, and RSS, so ProxyStream enum dispatch is not sufficient to explain the SOTA gap.

tokio_single_client_maxh1_candidate.command: PATH="$HOME/.cargo/bin:$PATH" python3 docs/benchmarks/run_https_proxy_bench.py --baseline libcurl --ylong-client async --ylong-concurrency-model single-client --scenario all --requests 200,1000 --repeats 3 --warmup 50 --concurrency 8 --bench-bin /tmp/ylong_http_target_tokio_single_client_maxh1_release/release/https_proxy_bench --result-dir docs/benchmarks/results/tokio-single-client-maxh1-candidate --figure-dir docs/benchmarks/results/tokio-single-client-maxh1-candidate
tokio_single_client_maxh1_candidate.finding: tokio_base materially improves CPU/request and some HTTPS-origin cells, but it still refutes SOTA. Throughput geomean is 1.031x, worst throughput is 0.790x in proxy-mTLS HTTPS-origin 1000 requests, p50/p95 still regress, and RSS remains above libcurl. Runtime substrate alone is not sufficient.

response_preread_diagnostic.command: PATH="$HOME/.cargo/bin:$PATH" python3 docs/benchmarks/run_https_proxy_bench.py --baseline libcurl --ylong-client async --ylong-concurrency-model single-client --scenario all --requests 200 --repeats 3 --warmup 50 --concurrency 1 --phase-timing --bench-bin /tmp/ylong_http_target_preread_diag_release/release/https_proxy_bench --result-dir docs/benchmarks/results/response-preread-diagnostic --figure-dir docs/benchmarks/results/response-preread-diagnostic
response_preread_diagnostic.finding: Response-head pre-read diagnostics show ylong_http_client reads the full 4096-byte response body during response-head decoding in every measured request. This confirms full-body pre-read is real, but the same run shows body_drain is already about 1us/request and the diagnostic still fails SOTA only in HTTP-over-HTTPS-proxy while HTTPS-origin scenarios are near parity or faster, so full pre-read storage/copy is not sufficient as a SOTA-scale root cause.

known_counterexamples_or_limits: Response-head pre-read is real but not sufficient for SOTA: ylong pre-reads the full 4096-byte body per request, yet body_drain is already roughly 1us/request and phase timing is single-concurrency-only.

Concurrent phase diagnostics at concurrency=8 remain a SOTA counterexample: throughput geomean is 0.960x, worst throughput is 0.670x, p50/p95/CPU/RSS gates fail, and response_read dominates the failing HTTPS-origin proxy cells.

OpenSSL SSL_MODE_RELEASE_BUFFERS is not a sufficient RSS or nested-TLS performance lever for this workload: the candidate failed with 0.968x throughput geomean, 0.652x worst throughput, p50/p95 regressions, 1.399x CPU geomean, and 1.182x max RSS, so it was rejected rather than retained.
The original concurrent bench_tls_io interpretation is superseded because global TLS counters were sampled once per worker and summed, inflating SSL_read and pending counts.

The corrected dynamic-budget no-diagnostic matrix is still a SOTA counterexample: throughput geomean is 0.988x, worst throughput is 0.657x, p50 max is 1.566x, p95 max is 1.371x, CPU geomean is 1.376x, and RSS max is 1.209x.

Server-side send/relay diagnostics are causal narrowing evidence, not a SOTA candidate: throughput geomean is 0.993x, worst throughput is 0.728x, p50/p95/CPU/RSS gates fail, and client response_read remains the larger cost.

tokio_base dynamic-budget is the best observed runtime-substrate candidate so far, but it remains a SOTA counterexample: 1.133x throughput geomean, 0.809x worst throughput, p50 max 1.270x, and p95 max 1.142x; RSS max 1.073x is above libcurl but within the hard threshold.

Explicit HTTP/1 flush before response read is rejected as a SOTA candidate: throughput geomean is 0.972x, worst throughput is 0.696x, p50 max is 1.466x, p95 max is 1.340x, CPU geomean is 1.330x, and RSS max is 1.180x.

ylong_base runtime worker=1 is rejected as a SOTA candidate: focused HTTPS-over-HTTPS-proxy throughput geomean is 0.985x, worst throughput is 0.863x, p50 max is 1.172x, and p95 max is 1.111x despite CPU/request geomean improving to 0.841x.
